| Done   | OP-MOD-001 | Modularization foundation and package-boundary guardrails             | Added the modularization foundation without changing public HTTP contracts, response shapes, authorization behavior, audit redaction, public-token behavior, repository behavior, dashboard copy/visual behavior, or worker queue behavior: architecture validation now enforces workspace import direction, blocks app source imports from package internals, ratchets broad web imports from `@open-practice/domain`, requires consistent package root exports, and can collect route declarations from registrar-owned subfiles; selector output now hydrates shared-package builds before downstream tests; `@open-practice/database` and `@open-practice/providers` expose explicit root exports, and `@open-practice/domain/calendar-models` plus `@open-practice/domain/contact-models` expose web-safe model subpaths; API route dependencies now include queue/object-storage/provider adapter ports while preserving `ApiRouteDependencies`; draft export and intake automation defaults moved to API bootstrap/test bootstrap; direct package-source test imports were removed; setup, provider-settings, audit, AI operational proposal, legal research artifact, conflict-check, document-assembly, draft, connector, jobs/email, inbound-email, legal-clinic, operational-view, portal-access, document, task, conversation-thread, signature, contact, intake-template, calendar credential, calendar event, auth, firm-settings, billing-controls, billing entries/time-expense, hosted payment request, billing invoice/manual-payment, intake-forms, trust-transfer request, ledger core, ledger review/reconciliation, public consultation intake CRUD/review, matter workspace read-model, and matter lifecycle write-path repository capabilities now live behind focused repository contract modules, and provider-settings, audit, AI operational proposal, legal research artifact, conflict-check, document-assembly, draft, connector, jobs/email, inbound-email, legal-clinic, operational-view, portal-access, document, task, conversation-thread, signature, contact, intake-template, calendar credential, calendar event, auth, firm-settings, billing-controls, billing entries/time-expense, hosted payment request, billing invoice/manual-payment, intake-forms, trust-transfer request, ledger core, ledger review/reconciliation, public consultation intake CRUD/review, matter workspace read-model, and matter lifecycle write-path Drizzle/memory implementation details now live in repository submodules; dashboard formatter constants/helpers and review-rail preference persistence moved under `_features/dashboard`; communications model types moved under `_features/communications`, connector operations model types plus client reload fetching moved under `_features/connectors`, billing/funds model types moved under `_features/billing`, document-processing model types moved under `_features/document-processing`, document-assembly model types moved under `_features/document-assembly`, calendar model types moved under `_features/calendar`, external-upload model types moved under `_features/external-uploads`, secure share-link model types plus status loading moved under `_features/share-links`, contacts dashboard model/helper types plus review/data-quality loading moved under `_features/contacts`, and email-delivery model types plus server loading moved under `_features/email-delivery` while `types.ts` keeps re-export compatibility; the stateless `DocumentAssemblyDashboardBlock`, `FirstMatterWorkspace`, `CalendarSection`, `ExternalUploadsSection`, `ShareLinksSection`, `BillingSection`, `DocumentsSection`, `SignaturesSection`, `AuditSection`, `DraftingSection`, and `TrustControlsSection` now live in focused dashboard components while create-matter/calendar/external-upload/share-link mutation state plus billing mutation/request-builder state, document-processing cache/filter/OCR queue state, contact data-quality mutation state, trust-controls loading/cache state, and dashboard active-section URL/focus/sessionStorage shell behavior now lives in `_features/dashboard/dashboard-shell-state.ts` while composed mutation state stays in `dashboard-client.tsx`; `page.tsx` now shares server API fetch/error helpers from `_shared/server-api.ts`; connector operations, audit projection, billing dashboard, calendar dashboard, operations/status loading, document-assembly workbench loading, external-upload status/list loading, contact review/data-quality loading, share-link status loading, email-delivery loading, communications inbox loading, intake form/pipeline/public consultation loading, legal-clinic loading, and legal-research loading moved to server-only feature resources while preserving optional `access_denied`/`unavailable`, billing access-denied semantics, calendar disabled-capability fallback semantics, email history fallback semantics, communications fallback semantics, intake/public-consultation status precedence, legal-clinic profile coercion, and legal-research disabled-capability fallback semantics; worker report, connector delivery, email delivery, OCR, and AI-triage jobs now live in queue-family processor modules with shared processor ports in `processors/types.ts`; public-consultation intake Drizzle row/insert mapping now lives in the public-consultation-intakes repository submodule while `drizzle-mappers.ts` keeps compatibility re-exports; and the first database schema split moved enum, core, matter, AI/research, provider-settings, legal-clinic, connector/integration, auth/session/MFA/passkey/recovery, jobs/email, tasks, calendar, contact, and saved operational view, conversation-thread, conflict-check, firm-settings, notification-preference, public-consultation, audit-event, billing-control, portal-link, and document-ingestion/media, inbound-email, drafting, signature-request, intake, document-assembly/signature-envelope, access-log, ledger, and billing tables into schema submodules while `schema.ts` is now a pure compatibility export aggregator. | Done   | OP-SEC-CRR | Code review remediation and proof-gate hardening                      | Merged the code-review remediation branch through `main`: local evidence directories are Docker-ignored and policy-checked; release artifact secret scanning reports large-file skip posture; public-token flows now support header-token transport with legacy path-token scrubbing; request logging redacts legacy public-token URLs; public share/upload status responses keep `enabled` as provider/config state and add permission-aware `canCreate`/`canManage`; receipt-token recording is matter/email-consistent and compare-and-set; email delivery metadata is sanitized; JSON-controlled booleans and upload fields are strict; mutation fetches clear busy states on network failure; selector/proof/Docker gates were tightened. metadata stay out of scope. creation, claim/reassign state, campaign automation, SMS delivery, bulk delivery, ad-spend ingestion, automatic client contact, schema changes, public routes, and queue persistence stay out of scope. | Done   | OP-T149    | Payment settlement webhook and reconciliation review V1               | Added the first payment settlement/reconciliation review slice: hosted payment-request shells can now record one authenticated staff-side normalized settlement event as `needs_review` processor posture with safe provider/event/session/status/amount cues, bounded evidence flags, route-audit metadata, and Billing dashboard review copy. The route does not accept raw webhook bodies, signing headers, card/customer details, checkout URLs, refund artifacts, or chargeback payloads, and it does not create manual payments, mutate invoice balances/status, create reconciliation records, reconcile deposits, handle refunds/chargebacks, vault cards, post trust entries, enable production Stripe, or expose a public provider webhook/replay endpoint. | Done   | OP-T151    | Legal research provider job boundary with citation review controls V1 | Added the first legal-research provider job boundary slice: `POST /api/legal-research/provider-jobs` records or queues a reserved matter-scoped `legal_research_provider_review` job on `ai_triage` with strict input limited to artifact IDs, source-type labels, jurisdiction, and counts. `GET /api/legal-research/workspace` now returns redacted provider-job summaries and citation-review controls, the worker skips the reserved job with safe metadata, and the Research dashboard renders the boundary without adding a generation UI. Prompts, source text, provider evidence, scraped authority storage, citation-verification claims, legal-advice automation, client/public research access, and downstream source-record mutation stay out of scope. | Done   | OP-T152    | Scoped developer API enforcement and webhook replay boundary          | Added the final active core-suite parity slice: developer app responses now expose registered-scope, subset-credential, reserved-rate-limit, and reserved-custom-action enforcement posture; webhook subscriptions require the registered `webhook.deliver` scope; and `POST /api/connectors/developer/apps/:appId/webhook-replays` provides an owner-only app-scoped replay boundary over failed/dead-letter connector outbox rows with active app, active matching subscription, expected-status confirmation, enabled connector, configured queue, redacted job metadata, and redacted audit evidence. Marketplace behavior, broad external API coverage, public developer auth, live payment-link API exposure, custom-action execution, raw webhook replay, inbound webhook recovery, and provider-specific recovery tools stay out of scope. proof](validation/OP-T152_SCOPED_DEVELOPER_API_WEBHOOK_REPLAY_PROOF_2026-06-04.md). | Done   | OP-T156    | Client portal permissioned workspace V2                               | Added the follow-up logged-in client portal workspace slice after OP-T144/OP-T145: portal grants now bind to client account users when accounts are set up, staff can grant/revoke explicit per-file `view_document` access for eligible safe matter documents, and `GET /api/client-portal/workspace` returns safe matter metadata, shared-file metadata, and signer-matched signature summaries. Client document reads return metadata only, client signature event routes record embedded viewed/completed/declined events for the matched signer, and the dashboard adds matter-contact document visibility controls. Raw downloads/previews, storage keys, token hashes, signing URLs, provider evidence, consent evidence, message bodies, live chat, SMS, checkout, broad document browsing, public-token rewrites, and object-storage behavior stayed out of scope. | Done   | OP-T146    | Task list and deadline review surface V1                              | Added the first staff-only task/deadline review slice: `GET /api/tasks/workbench` now returns a read-only `taskReview` projection over authorized task deadlines, visible matters, and OP-T131 scheduling request records with derived priority, assignment scope, privacy visibility, matter labels, scheduling review counts, and explicit false review-boundary flags. The Queues dashboard renders compact review counters and matter-linked review rows. Court-rule automation, provider sync, automatic deadline mutation, automatic reminder changes, queue delivery, automatic time-entry creation, client-visible deadline views, contact identifiers, and hidden cross-matter scheduling details stay out of scope. | Done   | OP-T145    | Client-visible bills and payment request workspace V1                 | Added the first client-visible billing workspace slice: `GET /api/client-portal/workspace` now returns a read-only `billing` projection over contact-matched issued, partially paid, and paid invoices plus existing hosted payment-request shell records. The API groups visible bills by granted matter, exposes only safe invoice number/status/date/amount/balance cues and payment-request status/delivery/reminder/plan placeholders, and the web client portal renders read-only billing summary/cards. Draft, approved, void, and other-contact invoices, invoice memos, line narratives, hosted paths, checkout URLs, external session IDs, processor objects, payment evidence, settlement, refunds, chargebacks, trust posting, payment-plan enforcement, and invoice-balance mutation stay out of scope. IDs, checkout URLs, payment settlement, realtime chat, SMS, and automatic client-update sends out of scope.                                                                                                               |
| Done   | OP-T143    | Provider config encryption                                            | Added authenticated AES-256-GCM envelope encryption for `provider_settings.encrypted_config` with a versioned `opencfg:v1:` envelope, firm/provider-setting scoped AAD, key parsing for 32-byte base64/base64url/hex secrets, PostgreSQL-backed API/worker startup enforcement, repository plaintext-return behavior, and legacy plaintext read compatibility. Follow-up added configurable SSE-S3 `AES256` requests for server-owned draft-export writes, inbound-email raw/body/attachment object writes, staff document-upload intents/completions, public external-upload intents/completions, public intake-form upload intents/completions, and the Mailgun raw-MIME webhook initial raw object write while preserving response shapes and repository contracts. Broader PII, app-side object envelope encryption, billing/trust, TOTP, production key rotation, bucket policy, and proactive migration work stayed out of scope.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Done proofs recorded in [OP-T143 provider config encryption proof](validation/OP-T143_PROVIDER_CONFIG_ENCRYPTION_PROOF_2026-06-02.md), [OP-T143 object-storage encryption follow-up proof](validation/OP-T143_OBJECT_STORAGE_ENCRYPTION_FOLLOWUP_PROOF_2026-06-02.md), and [OP-T143 inbound raw MIME SSE follow-up proof](validation/OP-T143_INBOUND_RAW_MIME_SSE_FOLLOWUP_PROOF_2026-06-03.md).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Done   | OP-INMAIL  | Mailgun inbound email webhook                                         | Added the first signed inbound-email provider webhook adapter for Mailgun raw MIME: the public route validates `timestamp + token` HMAC signatures with a freshness window, resolves the single configured firm server-side, reads the enabled `inbound_email`/`mailgun` provider setting with JSON and legacy plaintext config compatibility, stores raw MIME in the parser-owned raw-object namespace, creates an idempotent `inbound_email` parser lifecycle row, and enqueues the existing parser worker while keeping raw storage keys and signing material out of responses. Follow-up added owner-only parser-job retry/dead-letter recovery controls with confirmation guards, stalled-job checks, redacted job summaries, and safe audit metadata. Other provider adapters, automatic document promotion, migrations, UI, and parser changes stayed out of scope.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Done proofs recorded in [OP-INMAIL Mailgun inbound email webhook proof](validation/OP_INBOUND_EMAIL_MAILGUN_WEBHOOK_PROOF_2026-06-03.md) and [OP-INMAIL replay recovery proof](validation/OP_INBOUND_EMAIL_REPLAY_RECOVERY_PROOF_2026-06-03.md).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Done   | OP-SEC     | Security remediation batch                                            | Hardened upload completion/object verification, public upload duplicate redaction, scan-state control, conflict/audit scoped visibility, non-enumerating passkey login options, outbound connector SSRF guardrails, local Docker bind defaults, browser-safe public upload origin, baseline web security headers, inbound-email role-posture follow-up, and the 2026-06-05 full-scan remediation for client-external aggregate denial, production CORS scoping, same-matter duplicate IDs, inbound-email redaction, audit sequencing, OCR scan gating, provider status RBAC, redacted stale guest-token access logging, duplicate checksum advisory locking, closed local service-image follow-up evidence, and pushed/pruned mainline consolidation of the full-scan, audit sequence, OCR scan-gating, and staff aggregate/CORS security lanes.       | Done proofs recorded in [security remediation proof](validation/OP_SECURITY_REMEDIATION_PROOF_2026-05-31.md), [June 2026 security review merge/prune proof](validation/OP_MAINLINE_SECURITY_MERGE_PRUNE_PROOF_2026-06-05.md). or adding backend routes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Done   | OP-T121    | Calendar reminder delivery jobs                                       | Added opt-in delayed reminder email jobs through the existing outbox confirmation boundary while preserving dashboard reminder records as the source of truth. The 2026-06-16 follow-up reconciles queued opt-in reminder delivery when staff cancel, delete, reschedule, or refresh pending matter reminders: stale queued email outbox rows become cancelled, queued lifecycle jobs become skipped, confirmation-backed reschedules/refreshes queue one fresh delayed delivery, and firm/client reminders remain dashboard-only. Attendee sync, iCalendar subscriptions, meeting-link delivery, provider sync, meeting media, non-email delivery, user-preference routing, and retroactive cancellation of already-started/sent jobs remain out of scope. | Done   | OP-T102    | Native guest-session controls                                         | Closed the hosted meeting control-plane slice in commit `c79ffff`: persistent meeting-session records, HMAC-hashed guest access records, staff create/read/open/lock/end/issue/admit/deny/revoke controls, and a status-only public guest-session page. Realtime media, signaling, chat, recordings, temporary uploads, and meeting-link delivery through the public page stayed out of scope. | Done   | OP-T104    | Trust statement import preview                                        | Added the smallest review-only API/domain slice: `POST /api/ledger/reconciliations/preview` accepts synthetic statement rows for an existing trust asset account, dedupes repeated rows by normalized date/amount/description/reference, proposes matches against existing trust-ledger entries, and returns a review-only no-posting policy marker without posting ledger entries, creating reconciliations, moving funds, or emitting audit events. Historical snapshots and completed-work proof live in [Archive](archive/README.md).

## Coordination Rules

- Update this file before starting tracked work if status, owner, blocker, or immediate next move changes.
- Keep rows concrete; do not add summary-only tasks.
- Keep implementation rows scoped to one subsystem unless a coordinated exception is recorded here.
- Promote candidate work to `Ready` only when the first slice, owning paths, and validation plan are clear.
- Record fresh validation output before moving a reviewed row to `Done`.
- Preserve completed validation proof in [Archive](archive/README.md) instead of the active board.
- Do not edit `/Users/bryan/projects/nonprofit-manager` from this workboard.
